clinical_criteria: At least 5 years of enrollment in (or regular contact with) the health care system
Diagnosis of herpes zoster or varicella zoster since their 40th birthday
Cancer of the blood or bone marrow during the 365 day period prior to the index date (exclusion criterion for cases)
Chemotherapy infusion during the 180 day period prior to the index date (exclusion criterion for cases)
No evidence of herpes zoster infection documented anywhere in the patient's chart (control definition)
algorithm_summary: The phenotype algorithm identifies herpes zoster cases and controls based on clinical criteria rather than specific diagnostic codes. Cases are defined as individuals 40+ years old with a diagnosis of herpes zoster or varicella zoster since their 40th birthday, with at least 5 years of healthcare system enrollment, excluding those with HIV, blood/bone marrow cancer within 365 days, or chemotherapy within 180 days of index date. Controls have no evidence of herpes zoster infection and meet similar enrollment requirements but only exclude HIV-positive individuals. The index date for cases is the earliest herpes zoster diagnosis after age 40. Manual chart validation looks for affirmative mentions of 'zoster', 'shingles', or 'zona' in narrative sections (not just problem lists) to confirm diagnoses.
